title: Afternoon
commentary: The oil shock intensifies—WTI rips to $92.43 (+14.1%), Brent $94.08 (+10.2%) as Iran conflict shows no signs of abating. Natural gas joins the party +8.7% to $3.26. Equities resume their slide: Dow -1.3% at 47,329, S&P -1.3% at 6,745, Russell small caps getting massacred at -2.6%. The midday bounce has evaporated. Crypto capitulation deepens: Bitcoin -4.5% at $68,004, Ethereum -5.1% at $1,967. Gold steady at $5,140 (+1.1%), silver +2.2%—the only winners in this bloodbath are energy and precious metals.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: WTI crude breaches $90 for the first time since 2022—now +11.4% at $90.24 as Iran conflict intensifies into Day 7. Brent rips to $92.54 (+8.4%) with no ceiling in sight. Equities consolidate losses: Dow -1.2% at 47,399, S&P -1.0% at 6,761, Nasdaq -0.8%—the recovery from morning lows has stalled. Precious metals the bright spot: gold +1.1% at $5,140, silver +2.1% at $84 as stagflation hedges find buyers. Crypto still bleeding: Bitcoin -3.9% to $68,460, Ethereum -5.0% at $1,968—the risk-off correlation remains tight.

title: Midday
commentary: Buyers emerge from the wreckage—Dow claws back to -1.1% at 47,442 from session lows of -1.9%, S&P recovers to -1.0% at 6,762 as dip-buyers cautiously step in. Oil holds near highs at $88.85 (+9.7%) but the bleeding has slowed; Brent backs off $91 to $90.89. Precious metals finally working: gold +1.2% at $5,145, silver +2.4% at $84.23 as safe-haven flows find a home. Crypto still underwater but off the lows: Bitcoin -3.7% at $68,570, Ethereum -5.5% at $1,978. Dollar weakens to 99.1 (-0.2%) as the jobs miss weighs; Fed cut expectations rising.

title: Morning Session
commentary: Carnage on Wall Street—Dow craters 900 points (-1.9%) to 47,052 as oil explodes to $88.94 (+9.8%) on Trump's Iran comments and February's shocking -92,000 payrolls print. S&P -1.6% at 6,724, Nasdaq -1.4%, Russell small caps obliterated -2.3%. Brent crude breaches $91 for first time since 2022; the stagflation trade is ON. Crypto in freefall: Bitcoin -4.0% to $68,406, Ethereum -5.4% at $1,980 as risk assets capitulate. Gold finally catches a bid +0.7% at $5,121 as safe-haven flows rotate away from dollar (-0.1% at 99.24). This is what panic looks like.

title: Market Open
commentary: Oil explodes to $87.24 (+7.7%) as Iran conflict enters Day 7 with fresh missile strikes across the Gulf—Brent touches $90. Futures opened flat but weakness brewing after a shocking jobs report: US payrolls fell 92,000 in February, the worst drop since October. Dow 47,955 flat, S&P 6,831 unchanged, but the tape is confused—bad jobs data should mean Fed cuts, but $90 oil screams inflation. Crypto bleeding: Bitcoin -2.6% to $69,333, Ethereum -3.1% at $2,027 as risk assets can't find their footing. Dollar holds firm at 99.34; gold flat near $5,088 as the safe-haven trade fractures.

title: After Hours
commentary: A furious last-hour rally salvages what could have been far worse—Dow closes down 800 points (-1.6%) at 47,949, recovering 300 points from session lows as oil pulls back from $81 to $79.76 (+6.8%). S&P 500 claws back to -0.6% at 6,830, Nasdaq barely red at -0.3%. The late bid suggests de-escalation hopes persist, but gold can't catch a bid near $5,078 (-1.1%) as the surging dollar (+0.3% at 99.11) dominates safe-haven flows. Crypto stabilizes after the selloff: Bitcoin $71,207 (-2%), Ethereum $2,092 (-3%) holding above session lows. The market's verdict: ugly, but not catastrophic.

title: Market Close
commentary: The Iran oil shock delivers its verdict—Dow closes down 1,077 points (-2.2%) at 47,671, S&P -1.2% at 6,785, worst day in three weeks as WTI crude breaches $81.62 (+9.3%). Safe havens implode counterintuitively: gold -1.4% at $5,067, silver crushed -3.0% as surging dollar (+0.6% at 99.32) siphons all defensive flows. Crypto correlates perfectly with risk: Bitcoin -2.3% at $70,975, Ethereum -4.8% at $2,075—the 'digital gold' narrative dead on arrival. The tape's message is brutal: when oil spikes and the dollar rips, nothing works.

title: Late Afternoon
commentary: $80 oil is here—WTI breaches the psychological level to $81.09 (+8.6%), highest since October, as Iran escalation enters a new phase. Dow collapses 1,053 points (-2.2%) to 47,695, S&P -1.3% at 6,780, Nasdaq -1.2%—yesterday's gains obliterated and then some. Russell 2000 carnage at -2.5%; small caps now deep in correction territory. Crypto no safe haven: Bitcoin -3.0% at $71,000, Ethereum -2.4% at $2,075 tracking equities tick for tick. Dollar holds +0.46% at 99.22—the only winner in a sea of red. Two hours to the bell and no sign of dip-buying.

title: Afternoon
commentary: The rout deepens—Dow now down 972 points (-2.0%) at 47,776, S&P -1.3% at 6,780, Nasdaq -1.2% as the Iran oil shock reverberates through everything. Russell 2000 in free fall at -2.6%—small caps utterly destroyed. Oil holds near highs at $79.57 (+6.6%), just pennies from $80; dollar +0.45% at 99.22 continues to crush risk assets. Crypto correlates with stocks for once: Bitcoin -3.3% at $70,809, Ethereum -3.1% at $2,059—the 'digital gold' narrative nowhere to be found. Gold can't rally either near $5,070 (-1.3%); nothing works when the dollar is a wrecking ball.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Full capitulation mode—Dow craters 772 points (-1.6%) to 47,976, erasing the entire week's gains as oil touches $80 for the first time since October (+6.2% at $79.32, intraday high $79.97). S&P -0.65% at 6,825, Russell 2000 crushed -1.9% with small caps in freefall. Dollar surges +0.5% to 99.23—a wrecking ball for everything else; gold can't find a bid near $5,085 (-1.0%) despite peak fear. Crypto bleeding out: Bitcoin -2.5% to $71,341, Ethereum -2.1% at $2,080. This is what stagflation fears look like—oil up, everything else down.
